elements.short: A Map of relationships between 2 structures that can be used to transform data
elements.definition: A Map of relationships between 2 structures that can be used to transform data.

elements.short: Logical id of this artifact
elements.definition: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

elements.short: Metadata about the resource
elements.definition: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

elements.short: A set of rules under which this content was created
elements.definition: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

elements.short: Language of the resource content
elements.definition: The base language in which the resource is written.

elements.short: Text summary of the resource, for human interpretation
elements.definition: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

elements.short: Contained, inline Resources
elements.definition: These resources do not have an independent existence apart from the resource that contains them - they cannot be identified independently, and nor can they have their own independent transaction scope.

elements.short: Additional content defined by implementations
elements.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

elements.short: Extensions that cannot be ignored
elements.definition:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource and that modifies the understanding of the element that contains it and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer is allowed to define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

elements.short: Canonical identifier for this structure map, represented as a URI (globally unique)
elements.definition: An absolute URI that is used to identify this structure map when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which at which an authoritative instance of this structure map is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the structure map is stored on different servers.

elements.short: Additional identifier for the structure map
elements.definition: A formal identifier that is used to identify this structure map when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

elements.short: Business version of the structure map
elements.definition: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the structure map when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the structure map author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence.

elements.short: Name for this structure map (computer friendly)
elements.definition: A natural language name identifying the structure map. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

elements.short: Name for this structure map (human friendly)
elements.definition: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the structure map.

elements.short: draft | active | retired | unknown
elements.definition: The status of this structure map. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

elements.short: For testing purposes, not real usage
elements.definition: A Boolean value to indicate that this structure map is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

elements.short: Date last changed
elements.definition: The date  (and optionally time) when the structure map was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the structure map changes.

elements.short: Name of the publisher (organization or individual)
elements.definition: The name of the organization or individual that published the structure map.

elements.short: Contact details for the publisher
elements.definition: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

elements.short: Natural language description of the structure map
elements.definition: A free text natural language description of the structure map from a consumer's perspective.

elements.short: The context that the content is intended to support
elements.definition: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate structure map instances.

elements.short: Intended jurisdiction for structure map (if applicable)
elements.definition: A legal or geographic region in which the structure map is intended to be used.

elements.short: Why this structure map is defined
elements.definition: Explanation of why this structure map is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

elements.short: Use and/or publishing restrictions
elements.definition: A copyright statement relating to the structure map and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the structure map.

elements.short: Structure Definition used by this map
elements.definition: A structure definition used by this map. The structure definition may describe instances that are converted, or the instances that are produced.

elements.short: Unique id for inter-element referencing
elements.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

elements.short: Additional content defined by implementations
elements.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

elements.short: Canonical reference to structure definition
elements.definition: The canonical reference to the structure.

elements.short: source | queried | target | produced
elements.definition: How the referenced structure is used in this mapping.

elements.short: Name for type in this map
elements.definition: The name used for this type in the map.

elements.short: Documentation on use of structure
elements.definition: Documentation that describes how the structure is used in the mapping.

elements.short: Other maps used by this map (canonical URLs)
elements.definition: Other maps used by this map (canonical URLs).

elements.short: Named sections for reader convenience
elements.definition: Organizes the mapping into manageable chunks for human review/ease of maintenance.

elements.short: Human-readable label
elements.definition: A unique name for the group for the convenience of human readers.

elements.short: Another group that this group adds rules to
elements.definition: Another group that this group adds rules to.

elements.short: none | types | type-and-types
elements.definition: If this is the default rule set to apply for the source type or this combination of types.

elements.short: Additional description/explanation for group
elements.definition: Additional supporting documentation that explains the purpose of the group and the types of mappings within it.

elements.short: Named instance provided when invoking the map
elements.definition: A name assigned to an instance of data. The instance must be provided when the mapping is invoked.

elements.short: Specified minimum cardinality

elements.short: Specified maximum cardinality (number or *)
elements.definition: Specified maximum cardinality for the element - a number or a "*". This is optional; if present, it acts an implicit check on the input content (* just serves as documentation; it's the default value).

elements.definition: Specified type for the element. This works as a condition on the mapping - use for polymorphic elements.

elements.short: Default value if no value exists
